div1.entryFree:
Consort, subst., 1) with the accent on the last syll., company, fellowship: will thou be of our c.? Gentl. IV, 1, 64. he was of that c. Lr. II, 1, 99.
2) with the accent on the first syll., a company of musicians playing together (M. Edd. concert): visit by night your lady's chamber-window with some sweet c. Gentl. III, 2, 84. screech-owls make the c. full, H6B III, 2, 327 (cf. sort).
